title: Executive Order 10483—Establishing the Operations Coordinating Board

flags.justification: A multi-member board (Under Secretary of State as chair, Deputy Secretary of Defense, FOA Director, DCI, a presidential representative) reporting to the National Security Council — genuinely distributed, not concentrated.
Clear reporting chain to the National Security Council, with periodic reports required (Sec. 2).
Single coherent purpose: coordinating implementation of approved national security policy.
A purely advisory/coordinative body with no binding compliance regime against third parties.
No compliance regime exists for an incentive structure to attach to.
A standing coordination body for an ongoing government function (national security policy implementation), evergreen by nature, not tied to a specific emergency.
Sec. 6 explicitly preserves other agencies' existing functions, naming the Office of Defense Mobilization specifically.
A coordination body with no third-party compliance regime.

Establishes a significant new durable interagency layer in the national security policy-implementation apparatus, replacing the Psychological Strategy Board — a meaningful institutional precedent for how security policy coordination gets structured going forward.
Sec. 4's abolition and winding-up of the Psychological Strategy Board is a clean, explicit succession; Sec. 6 explicitly protects other agencies' authority.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (positive). Structural weight: 1/14 = 7.1%. Predecessor structure to what eventually evolved into the modern NSC staff coordination apparatus.
